ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Compliance With Order For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Continued To Aug-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melvin Hodges for Lottie Steib (Petitioner).

The Court stated that it is waiting for completing of an already ordered final distribution and questions why should the last four of the Beneficiaries bear the brunt of the shortfall. Mr. Hodges stated that a clawback is incomprehensible and impossible, that most of the Beneficiaries are people that emerged only when they found out they were Heirs, that there won't be any money, and that the person administering the Estate got statutory fees and is an Heir. Mr. Hodges also stated that the Heirs were advised they would be receiving less than what they were told they were going to get, that they want their money, that he has four declarations, that the calculations were made by the Examiner's office, and that the declarations are in agreement and authorize reducing the amounts. In addition, Mr. Hodges stated that the Beneficiaries don't care if others get more than them, that want what ever amount it is, that he can provide a proposed order today and that the amount of the shortfall is approximately $2,100.00. Furthermore, Mr. Hodges stated that he met with Margaret's Heirs and that they were under the opinion they weren't going to get anything. The Court stated that the Examiner's office reviews what is presented to them, questions why the Personal Representative does not make up the shortfall, stated that it does not think this is fair to the Heirs, that it is happy to look at any alternatives Counsel may have, and that it seems if the four Beneficiaries are 3.27% of the Estate, they should not brunt no more that 3.27% of the short fall. Status on compliance with order for final distribution is continued to August 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel needs to submit an order or an amended request if he chooses.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Mr. Hodges stated that while the examiner's notes referenced the date of death appraisal, the appraisal attached to his declaration by the probate referee was a subsequent valuation done within six months and was a correct valuation of the property. Ms. Williams stated that the subsequent valuation wwas attached to the May 6th filing before the court, and requested that the Court go forward with the sale today. She said that the beneficiaries consent to the sale and waive further notice, that the notice of sale has been published twice, that no objections to the dsale have been filed, and that there was no reason for the sale not to proceed today.
